name: Cloudflare Scoped API Token
description: Creates a scoped API token in Cloudflare. Unlike the legacy Global API Key, scoped tokens follow least-privilege by letting you restrict permissions to specific zones, accounts, and actions.

steps.title: Open the Cloudflare API Tokens page
steps.instruction: Sign in to the Cloudflare dashboard and navigate to your profile's API Tokens page.

steps.title: Click 'Create Token'
steps.instruction: Click the 'Create Token' button. You will see a list of token templates and a 'Create Custom Token' option at the bottom.
steps.screenshot_hint: Blue 'Create Token' button at the top of the API Tokens section

steps.title: Choose a template or create a custom token
steps.instruction: Cloudflare offers templates such as 'Edit zone DNS', 'Read analytics', and 'WordPress'. If one matches your use case, click 'Use template' next to it. Otherwise, scroll to the bottom and click 'Get started' under 'Create Custom Token' for full control over permissions.
steps.screenshot_hint: List of token templates with 'Use template' buttons, and a 'Create Custom Token' section at the bottom

steps.title: Name the token
steps.instruction: Enter a descriptive name for the token, for example 'rialu-dns-edit' or 'deploy-worker-token'. Good names include the purpose and scope so you can identify the token later.

steps.title: Configure permissions
steps.instruction: Under 'Permissions', add the specific permission grants this token needs. Each permission has three parts: the resource category (Account, Zone, or User), the resource type (e.g. DNS, Workers Scripts, Zone Settings), and the access level (Read or Edit). Add only the permissions you actually need — for example, 'Zone / DNS / Edit' for DNS record management.
steps.warning: Follow the principle of least privilege. Do not grant Edit access where Read is sufficient, and scope to specific zones rather than 'All zones' when possible.

steps.title: Restrict zone resources
steps.instruction: Under 'Zone Resources', choose which zones this token can access. Select 'Include / Specific zone' and pick the zone(s) you need, or 'All zones' if the token must operate across your entire account.
steps.screenshot_hint: Dropdown for 'Include' or 'Exclude' with a zone picker

steps.title: Optionally restrict by IP
steps.instruction: Under 'Client IP Address Filtering', you can restrict the token to only work from specific IP addresses or CIDR ranges. This is useful for server-to-server tokens with a known egress IP.

steps.title: Optionally set a TTL
steps.instruction: Under 'TTL', you can set a start and end date for the token. After the end date, the token automatically stops working. Leave blank for no expiration.

steps.title: Review and create
steps.instruction: Click 'Continue to summary' to review the token's permissions, zone scope, and any IP or TTL restrictions. Confirm everything is correct, then click 'Create Token'.
steps.screenshot_hint: Summary page showing permission grants and a 'Create Token' button

steps.title: Copy the token
steps.instruction: Cloudflare displays the token value on the confirmation page. Copy it immediately using the copy button or by selecting the text.
steps.warning: Cloudflare only shows the token once. If you leave this page without copying, you must delete the token and create a new one.

steps.title: Verify the token works
steps.instruction: Cloudflare provides a test command on the same page. You can also run: curl -s -H 'Authorization: Bearer <your-token>' https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/user/tokens/verify — a successful response will show '"status": "active"'.
steps.screenshot_hint: Terminal showing JSON with status 'active' and a success result

vault_entry.notes: Scoped API token. Check permissions summary in Cloudflare dashboard for exact grants.
